member.name: Clive Lewis
member.synopsis: Clive Lewis is the Labour MP for Norwich South , and has been an MP continually since 7 May 2015.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Business: Regulation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, in relation to his Department’s review of Responsible Business Conduct, if he will (i) set out the process and timeline for completion and (ii) whether he plans to publish the review.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The review has been completed and Government is considering its findings. We will publish the review and set out our plans soon.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business and Trade

writtenQuestions.heading: Palantir
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether his Department was informed that staff of Palantir Technologies UK Limited and external consultants had been granted administrative roles providing unrestricted access to identifiable patie
writtenQuestions.answerText: The NHS Federated Data Platform (NHS FDP) safely connects information from different systems across the National Health Service into a single, secure environment. This allows staff to co-ordinate care better to improve outcomes for patients. The NHS FDP...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: HMP Wormwood Scrubs: Health Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment he has made of the adequacy of healthcare provision for prisoners with neuromuscular and other progressive conditions at HMP Wormwood Scrubs; and what steps he is taking to ensure t
writtenQuestions.answerText: Whilst no specific assessment has been made, individuals with neuromuscular or progressive conditions are assessed on a case-by-case basis as there is significant variation with regard to how they may present and this is somewhat of an umbrella term.Typic...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Water: Regulation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what assessment he has made of the potential merits of ensuring a nationwide presence of offices for the new water regulator in England.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Defra is working with stakeholders, including existing water regulators, on the strategic design of the new regulator to ensure it is able to support delivery of the Government’s vision set out in the Water White Paper. A range of options for its operatin...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Water: Regulation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, whether steps are being taken to provide staff transferring to a new water regulator in England with (a) a single set of terms and conditions, (b) access to the Civil Service Pension S
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department will follow all Transfer of Undertakings (Protection of Employment) legislation or Cabinet Office Statement of Practice regulations as the details regarding the creation of the new regulator are finalised.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Water Companies
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, whether there were (a) formal minutes and (b) formal agendas for the meetings held between Ministers and Water company CEOs for the last three months of 2025.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Ministers meet regularly with a range of stakeholders, including Water Company CEOs. Details of ministerial meetings with external organisations and individuals are published quarterly in arrears on GOV.UK
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Water: Regulation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what recent discussions she has had with trade unions on the proposed new water regulator for England.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Minister Hardy met with Defra Group Trade Unions on the 16 April to discuss how the Department will continue to engage with Trade Unions on the programme of water reforms.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Midwives
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to (a) support the recruitment and training of midwives and (b) increase the availability of employment opportunities for newly qualified midwives.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We are working with NHS England and the Nursing and Midwifery Council to ensure that midwifery training consistently delivers modern maternity care that respects a woman’s choice and individual circumstances. We are also introducing a new set of professio...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department for Science, Innovation and Technology (DSIT) has committed a record £58.5 billion investment in R&D over the next four years, including £38.6 billion allocated to UK Research and Innovation (UKRI).The Science and Technology Facilities ...

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department for Science, Innovation and Technology (DSIT) has committed a record £58.5 billion investment in R&D over the next four years, including £38.6 billion allocated to UK Research and Innovation (UKRI).The Science and Technology Facilities ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Equality and Human Rights Commission: Codes of Practice
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for Women and Equalities, what steps she is taking to include Trans safety in the implementation of the updated EHRC Code of Practice.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government received an updated draft Code of Practice from the EHRC on 13 April, and, whilst we cannot comment on the draft Code, we intend to lay it in Parliament in May. We have always been clear that duty bearers should set their policies in line w...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Women and Equalities

writtenQuestions.heading: Equality and Human Rights Commission: Codes of Practice
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for Women and Equalities, how she plans to support businesses groups who wish to remain trans inclusive in the context of the updated EHRC Code of Practice.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government received an updated draft Code of Practice from the EHRC on 13 April, and, whilst we cannot comment on the draft Code, we intend to lay it in Parliament in May. We have always been clear that duty bearers should set their policies in line w...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Women and Equalities

writtenQuestions.heading: Palantir: Contracts
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what is the content of the advice he has been given on the contract and performance of Palantir and the Federated Data Platform, particularly in relation to triggering the break clause in March 202
writtenQuestions.answerText: Ministers in the Department have been apprised of the nature of the NHS Federated Platform contract, that the seven-year contract term is split into an initial three-year term, plus extension options of two years, plus one year and one year, and that the ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Biometrics
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, whether her Department has issued guidance on using experimental methods in conjunction with facial recognition.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Home Office has not issued specific guidance. Police use of facial recognition and similar technologies is currently governed by a legal framework that includes data protection, equality and human rights laws, national guidance, a code of practice and...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Police Custody: Photographs
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what assessment she has made of the potential merits of introducing a statutory requirement for the deletion of custody images where no (a) charge and (b) conviction follows.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The retention and deletion of custody images is currently governed by the statutory Police and Information Management Code of Practice, and the associated College of Policing’s Management of Police Information (MoPI) guidance and Authorised Professional P...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Shivat Zion
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, if she will take steps to impose sanctions on the CEO of Israeli charity Shivat Zion, Shraga Evers, and the charity's official, Jonathan Vigné, in the context of support for
writtenQuestions.answerText: The situation in the West Bank continues to be a priority for the UK. It is the UK's longstanding policy not to speculate on potential sanctions designations.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Legal Aid Scheme: Norwich South
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, what assessment his Department has made of the adequacy of face-to-face civil legal aid provision for the residents of Norwich South constituency; when the provision was last reviewed; and what steps his Departme
writtenQuestions.answerText: Legal aid is a vital part of the justice system and underpins our plans to build a justice system that works fairly for all parties.The Legal Aid Agency (LAA) continuously assesses the available supply of legal aid services throughout England and Wales ac...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Justice

writtenQuestions.heading: NHS: Palantir
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether he has sought advice on triggering the break clause in Palantir’s contract with the NHS.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The current contract for the NHS Federated Data Platform is for seven years, ending in 2031, with the initial term ending March 2027. Advice is given regularly on the contract and performance, and ministers have been given advice on the need for a decisio...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: ME/CFS: Health Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether an impact assessment has been conducted of delaying consideration of a specialised commissioned service until April 2027 on patients with Myalgic Encephalomyelitis (also known as Chronic Fa
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government published the ME/CFS Final Delivery Plan in July 2025, which is available at the following link:https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/mecfs-the-final-delivery-planThe plan focuses on three main areas to improve care and support for tho...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Lord Mandelson
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, whether (a) he or (b) any members of his Department met with or corresponded with Lord Mandelson on Palantir.
writtenQuestions.answerText: In line with the Humble Address motion agreed on 4 February, any information regarding Mr Mandelson's appointment to, employment in, and withdrawal from, his posting as His Majesty's Ambassador to the United States will be published according to the proce...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Defence
